sections.title: DEMOCRACY UNDER ATTACK
sections.subtitle: Gerrymandering, voter suppression, electoral college distortion, filibuster abuse, and the systematic dismantling of democratic institutions.
sections.narrative: American democracy faces structural attacks on multiple fronts. Gerrymandering lets politicians choose their voters (North Carolina went from 10-3 to 7-6 after courts intervened). The Electoral College has installed 5 minority-vote presidents. The filibuster lets 1 senator block 99. Since 2011, over 1,688 voting restrictions have been introduced across 49 states. Project 2025 provides the blueprint for institutional capture. Understanding these mechanisms is the first step to defending against them.

sections.title: ECONOMIC REALITY vs. ECONOMIC MYTHOLOGY
sections.subtitle: Trickle-down debunked, wealth hoarding mechanics, corporate welfare, union busting, and the data on what actually grows economies.
sections.narrative: A 50-year LSE study across 18 countries found zero evidence that tax cuts for the rich stimulate growth -- the only measurable effect was that the top 1%'s income share doubled. Meanwhile, corporate welfare costs $92B/year (vs $70B for the entire social safety net). Three Americans own more wealth than the bottom 50% combined. Piketty's r>g formula shows why: capital returns 4-5% while economic growth averages 1-1.5%, mathematically guaranteeing concentration. Progressive economic policies aren't ideology -- they're what the data supports.

sections.title: INEQUALITY & CORPORATE POWER
sections.subtitle: Wealth concentration, regulatory capture, dark money, lobbying, media consolidation, and how corporations write their own rules.
sections.narrative: Six corporations control 90% of US media. Koch networks spend $889M per election cycle. Leonard Leo's network captured 6 of 9 Supreme Court seats and 80% of Trump's appeals court judges. ALEC has pushed 2,900 model bills across all 50 states, with 600+ becoming law. Donors Trust launders $165M/year in untraceable dark money. The right-wing funding machine dwarfs progressive organizing -- partially because fewer scruples means fewer norms stopping them from breaching democratic guardrails.

sections.title: THE CULTURE WAR MACHINE
sections.subtitle: Manufactured outrage, wedge issues, moral panics, and how cultural grievance is weaponized to distract from economic policy.
sections.narrative: Culture wars serve a specific political function: they redirect working-class anger away from economic policy and toward cultural scapegoats. The Southern Strategy pioneered this -- Lee Atwater explained it openly in 1981. Today's version uses trans rights, CRT, immigration, and 'woke' as triggers to activate tribal identity while tax cuts, deregulation, and corporate welfare pass unnoticed. The pattern is consistent: every culture war panic peaks during legislative sessions that benefit the donor class.

sections.title: EVERY ACCUSATION IS A CONFESSION
sections.subtitle: The GOP projection pattern: anti-gay lawmakers caught gay, 'protect the children' advocates convicted of child crimes, Dark Triad psychology, and the data on who actually commits the crimes they accuse others of.
sections.narrative: From 1961-2016, Republican administrations produced 142 criminal indictments vs 2 for Democrats -- a 71:1 ratio. At least 11 prominent anti-gay Republican politicians were caught in gay scandals. Multiple 'protect the children' advocates -- including Dennis Hastert (Speaker of the House) -- were convicted of child sex crimes. This isn't coincidence: Dark Triad personality research shows sociopaths systematically assume others are as dishonest as they are, making projection their default communication strategy. The accusation reveals the accuser.

sections.title: MEDIA, MISINFORMATION & PROPAGANDA
sections.subtitle: Media consolidation, Sinclair 'must-run' scripts, Fox News as political operation, social media manipulation, and the deception pipeline.
sections.narrative: In 1983, 50 companies controlled US media. Today it's 6. Sinclair Broadcasting requires local stations to air 'must-run' conservative segments that appear as local news. Fox News was founded explicitly as a political operation. Social media algorithms amplify outrage because engagement = revenue. The result: millions of Americans live in information ecosystems designed to produce specific political outcomes, not informed citizens. Understanding the pipeline from think tank to talking point to viral outrage is essential for progressive communication strategy.

sections.title: POLICY: WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS
sections.subtitle: Healthcare, education, infrastructure, housing, climate -- the evidence base for progressive policy positions.
sections.narrative: Universal healthcare systems cost 30-50% less per capita than the US system while delivering better outcomes. Countries with strong public education systems have higher economic mobility. Infrastructure investment returns $1.50-$2.20 for every dollar spent. The progressive policy platform isn't idealism -- it's what the data from every other developed nation shows actually works. The challenge isn't evidence; it's overcoming the multi-billion dollar messaging machine that argues otherwise.

sections.title: GLOBAL AUTHORITARIANISM & HISTORICAL PARALLELS
sections.subtitle: Project 2025, Gleichschaltung parallels, democratic backsliding worldwide, Trump copycats, and what history tells us about this moment.
sections.narrative: Project 2025 mirrors historical authoritarian consolidation playbooks. The Gleichschaltung comparison (Nazi coordination of institutions) isn't hyperbole -- it's structural analysis. Globally, democratic backsliding is accelerating: Hungary, Turkey, Brazil (under Bolsonaro), India, and the Philippines all followed similar patterns. Trump has spawned copycats -- Poilievre in Canada, Milei in Argentina. The tariff capitulation pattern shows smaller nations bowing to authoritarian economic pressure. History shows these patterns are recognizable, resistible, but only if recognized early enough.

entries.summary: Details the public rift between Donald Trump and Elon Musk, focusing on the Department of Government Efficiency and government contracts.
keyFacts: Musk reportedly invested $290 million into Trump's 2024 campaign.
The U.S. government lacks the capacity to launch satellites at the same rate as SpaceX, creating a critical dependency.
Mentions a potential 'bombshell' allegation by Musk regarding Trump's presence in Epstein files.
biasFlags: Includes unverified speculation regarding Epstein file contents

entries.source: The Reason People Are Fed up With Democrats
entries.summary: Robert Reich argues that 45 years of stagnant wages for 80% of Americans have fueled a powerful anti-establishment movement.
keyFacts: The bottom 60-80% of Americans have seen no real wage increase in nearly half a century.
Trump utilizes 'cultural populism' symbols to distract from policies like tax cuts for the rich.
Urges Democrats to embrace 'economic populism' to address the reality of rigged systems.

entries.source: AOC Asks MAGA SHOCK Question the Results are STUNNING!
entries.summary: Analyzes an AOC Instagram Q&A where Trump voters shared their reasons for regret and their initial perceptions of Trump as a working-class champion.
keyFacts: Voters cited 'conservative media echo chambers' as a primary reason for initial support.
Regrets were often tied to Trump targeting non-criminals for deportation despite campaign promises.
Notes that Trump effectively attacks checks and balances (courts, universities, press) to consolidate power.

entries.source: Democrats Learn The Wrong Lessons From Zohran
entries.summary: Critiques centrist 'Project 2029' initiatives for attempting to capture progressive energy while rejecting progressive policies like tax redistribution.
keyFacts: Zoran Mamdani won the NYC mayoral primary on a platform of rent freezes and universal childcare.
Centrists are described as being 'trapped' by the need to satisfy corporate donors.
Argues that authenticity—believing in one's own policy—is the primary driver of electoral success for the left.
biasFlags: Strongly anti-centrist/left-wing bias

entries.source: What Makes Trump + MAGA So Cruel? A Psychiatrist Explains
entries.summary: Explores 'social status anxiety' as the psychological driver for the MAGA movement, particularly among white males feeling 'dethroned.'
keyFacts: The primary fear is cultural displacement rather than economic poverty.
MAGA supporters 'merge' with Trump's perceived victimhood, viewing his legal losses as their own.
The movement is described as being willing to dismantle democracy to restore traditional social hierarchies.
biasFlags: Pathologizes political support as a manifestation of collective trauma

entries.source: Why You Keep Falling For BULLSH*T
entries.summary: Argues that modern disinformation weaponizes scientific uncertainty to create 'manufactured doubt' for political and corporate gain.
keyFacts: Traces the strategy to a 1969 tobacco memo stating 'Doubt is our product.'
Identifies 'epistemic warfare' where actors say 'concerns have been raised' to paralyze public action.
Differentiates between legitimate scientific uncertainty and industry-funded doubt used to delay climate or health policy.

entries.source: Why Zohran Mamdani Won - Norman Finkelstein Breaks It Down
entries.summary: Norman Finkelstein analyzes the Mamdani victory as a sign of hope for an economically precarious generation of young people.
keyFacts: Estimates that 80% of the population faces precarious economic realities.
The campaign succeeded despite a 'total media assault' and billionaire-funded opposition.
Connects the George Floyd demonstrations to a broader 'futureless future' shared by young ethnically diverse voters.
biasFlags: Strong ideological support for the candidate

entries.source: NYC Mayoral Candidate Zohran Mamdani's Dinner Party Interview
entries.summary: Mamdani outlines a platform of 'municipal ownership' to combat poverty, including city-run grocery stores and expanded public housing.
keyFacts: Proposes building housing on underutilized city-owned vacant lots and parking areas.
Advocates for 'clubhouse' mental health models that have been shown to reduce hospitalizations.
A 2023 pilot making one bus route free in each borough led to a 38% ridership increase.

entries.source: The Right-Wings Most Insidious 2025 Lies - SOME MORE NEWS
entries.summary: Deconstructs 'foundational lies' used by the GOP to justify the dismantling of Social Security, the FDA, and judicial checks on power.
keyFacts: Debunks the claim that vaccines contain 'aborted fetus debris'; MMR used fetal cell lines from the 1960s in development only.
Exposes a faked study regarding the abortion pill Mifepristone that included non-abortion uses to inflate side-effect data.
Refutes claims about Social Security insolvency with actual trust fund data.

entries.source: Eve Lancer on Canada's NDP and imperialism
entries.summary: Critiques Canada's NDP for supporting NATO interventionism and failing to oppose Western imperialism, particularly regarding Haiti.
keyFacts: Critiques the NDP's support for the 'core group' determining Haitian policy.
Faces criminal harassment charges for online criticism of a Zionist figure, which he claims is a 'lawfare' tactic.
biasFlags: Explicitly anti-Zionist and anti-imperialist framing

entries.source: How half the US lost part of their minds
entries.summary: Explores the 'lead-crime hypothesis,' linking historical leaded gasoline exposure to cognitive decline and the violent crime wave of the 20th century.
keyFacts: Estimated 170 million Americans experienced significant childhood lead exposure, losing a combined 800 million IQ points.
Lead mimics calcium, disrupting signaling in the nervous system and permanently altering brain development.
States with the largest decreases in lead exposure post-1970 saw the most significant drops in violent crime 22 years later.

entries.source: A CEO Wanted to Run Healthcare Like Taco Bell. Heres How His Patients Are Doing
entries.summary: Exposes the profit-driven 'fast food' model of dialysis providers DaVita and Fresenius, which control 80% of the U.S. market.
keyFacts: American dialysis patients have a 20% annual mortality rate, compared to 6% in Japan.
Clinics allegedly avoid calling ambulances for emergencies to protect their Medicare accountability scores.
Short-staffing leads to 'high ultrafiltration rates' that can cause sudden drops in blood pressure and cardiac disease.

entries.source: SOUTH PARK IS REAL HOPECORE
entries.summary: Interprets a South Park episode as a sign that 'anti-woke' identity has become too mainstream to remain a cool counterculture, prompting a shift in discourse.
keyFacts: Notes that podcasters like Joe Rogan and Theo Von are increasingly hosting diverse political perspectives.
Suggests that 'kindness' is becoming the new 'punk rock' as a reaction to internet cruelty.
Argues that the previous delivery of 'woke' messages was often too scolding, which triggered defensive responses.
biasFlags: Subjective cultural analysis presented as a definitive trend

entries.source: Should We Trust (Neoliberal) Economists?
entries.summary: Critiques the field of economics as a non-neutral discipline used to justify wealth concentration and provide 'tools of violence' against the poor.
keyFacts: Highlights the failure of the 'Chicago Boys' in Pinochet's Chile, where neoliberal reforms led to an economic crash.
Argues that metrics like GDP are arbitrary and ignore non-monetary human well-being.
Fernando De Soto's property titling theory is cited as a failure that increased rents and concentrated land in fewer hands.
biasFlags: Strong Marxist/Socialist framing of the entire discipline of economics

entries.source: Mexico lifts 8 million people out of poverty since 2022
entries.summary: Reports on the success of Mexican social programs and minimum wage increases in significantly reducing poverty and income inequality.
keyFacts: Mexico's minimum wage tripled from $4.75/day to $15/day between 2018 and 2025.
Extreme poverty dropped by 23% under President Lopez Obrador.
Foreign investment in Mexico hit record levels in 2024 despite fears that raising wages would deter capital.

entries.source: The 10 Core Myths Still Taught in Business Schools | Frankly 99
entries.summary: Critiques mainstream economic education for ignoring biophysical limits and the fundamental role of energy in economic output.
keyFacts: Myth: 'Price equals value.' Market prices ignore vital externalities like clean air and water.
Myth: 'Money is created from savings.' In reality, most money is created endogenously through bank debt.
The economy is a 'wholly owned subset' of the environment, making infinite growth physically impossible.

entries.source: The New Red Scare is Here
entries.summary: Claims that NSPM7 and other government directives are creating a modern 'Red Scare' to criminalize progressive dissent.
keyFacts: NSPM7 tasks defense/intelligence sectors with disrupting groups displaying 'indicators of violence,' including anti-capitalism.
Draws parallels to the 1950s McCarthyism and the Taft-Hartley Act's role in gutting the New Deal.
Notes that anything from universal healthcare to gender expression is being labeled a threat to 'Americanism.'
biasFlags: Uses hyperbolic 'Red Scare' framing to interpret current policy memorandums

entries.source: Trump Is NOT a Novelty! (w/ Yanis Varoufakis)
entries.summary: Yanis Varoufakis argues that Trump's foreign policy is an overt continuation of long-standing U.S. hegemonic strategies, specifically moving from a surplus to a deficit power that weaponizes the dollar.
keyFacts: Trump's 'Mara Lago paper' strategy treats the world as a 'bicycle wheel' where the U.S. negotiates with individual 'spokes' to prevent collective bargaining.
NATO is characterized as a 'protection racket' that creates tension to justify the military-industrial complex.
Argues the U.S. has 'lost the new cold war' with China and is now dismantling the globalist institutions it previously built.
biasFlags: Highly contrarian framing of international alliances as 'mafia' structures

entries.source: Two AI Agents Design a New Economy (Beyond Capitalism / Socialism)
entries.summary: Proposes 'adaptive mutualism,' a hybrid economic system that replaces the capitalism/socialism binary with a 10-step model focused on human flourishing within planetary boundaries.
keyFacts: Advocates for 'contextual coordination' where different goods (necessities vs. luxuries) use different allocation mechanisms (participation vs. markets).
Proposes global resource quotas with tradable rights for wealthy nations to ensure equity.
Innovation is decoupled from material throughput, redefining growth as qualitative improvement.
biasFlags: Utopian framing relies on theoretical AI simulations

entries.source: Middle Class CRUSHED as Billionaires Take EVERYTHING
entries.summary: Details the systemic barriers to wealth accumulation for the 99%, citing stagnant wages, rising startup costs, and tax laws that favor capital over labor.
keyFacts: Oxfam data: Two-thirds of new wealth created since 2020 went to the top 1%.
The top 1% of companies control 90% of the U.S. economy.
Start-up costs for new businesses have risen to an average of $40,000 for the first year, deterring low-capital entrepreneurs.

entries.source: Your resistance fantasy doesn't match reality.
entries.summary: Argues that non-violent resistance is twice as likely to succeed as violent resistance, emphasizing the need to 'gum up the works' through bureaucratic non-compliance.
keyFacts: Violent resistance often provides regimes with the justification for authoritarian crackdowns.
Successful democratic restoration usually involves a broad-based coalition of diverse people performing mundane acts of defiance.
Authoritarian takeovers move quickly to outrun their own unpopularity, making 'delay' a strategic victory for the opposition.

entries.source: Doctor Explains Why They're Terrified to Speak Out
entries.summary: Profiles Dr. Elisabeth Potter's legal battle with UnitedHealthcare after she exposed their practice of pressuring surgeons for patient data mid-operation.
keyFacts: Potter was targeted by law firm Clare Locke, which also represented the Sacklers and Russian oligarchs.
UnitedHealthcare allegedly retaliated by keeping Potter's surgery center out-of-network, threatening its financial survival.
Over $500,000 was raised via social media to keep Potter's center open following the viral dispute.

entries.source: The ONE THING You Can Do to Fight Fascism RIGHT NOW
entries.summary: Argues that fascism thrives on public fatigue and that the most radical act is taking small, consistent, incremental steps toward justice.
keyFacts: Waiting for a 'charismatic leader' to emerge is identified as a dangerous strategy that leads to paralysis.
Effective resistance includes local acts like raising bail funds, speaking at school boards, or starting mutual aid pods.
Fascism only survives through the inaction of the majority.

entries.source: The Hidden Truth About Anti-Work
entries.summary: Critiques the 40-hour work week as an industrial-era control mechanism that prioritizes output over human well-being and life quality.
keyFacts: Gallup data indicates 60% of workers feel emotionally detached from their jobs.
Distinguishes between 'task-oriented' labor (crafting, gardening) and 'time-oriented' labor (factory/office shifts).
Argues that the pursuit of consumption traps workers in a cycle of 'wage slavery' to afford unnecessary items.

entries.source: The Old Are Taking the World to the Grave - Prof Jiang Xueqin
entries.summary: Presents a theory of 'Gerontocracy,' arguing that Western decline is driven by the interests of wealthy, elderly individuals who prioritize safety and asset protection over innovation.
keyFacts: Elderly leaders are characterized as more likely to implement 'police state' surveillance due to inherent fears for personal safety.
Wealthy pensioners are identified as the primary beneficiaries of rising property prices and stock valuations.
Predicts the implementation of digital currencies to eliminate financial freedom for younger, more rebellious generations.
biasFlags: Contains ageist generalizations about the cognitive state of the elderly
Leans into conspiratorial themes regarding digital currencies

entries.source: How Chicago Labor History Teaches Us To Fight Fascism Today
entries.summary: Uses the Haymarket Massacre and Pullman Strike as historical frameworks to advocate for modern unionization and mass protest.
keyFacts: The 1886 Haymarket incident was a direct result of police intervention in a labor demonstration for the 8-hour workday.
The 1894 Pullman Strike failed partly because the union excluded Black workers, who were then used as strikebreakers.
Union membership is currently at historic lows due to the rise of gig work and aggressive corporate union-busting.

entries.source: What the Next 5 to 10 Years Look Like | Prof. Jiang Xueqin
entries.summary: Develops a 'Rise, Decline, and Collapse' model for societies, arguing that the Western world has moved from 'Deception' (Decline) into 'Coercion' (Collapse).
keyFacts: Rising societies operate on consent and open criticism; declining societies operate on bureaucracy and deception.
Predicts that Western governments will use foreign wars as a distraction to prevent internal revolt during economic collapse.
Authoritarian regimes become vulnerable to 'perfect storms' because they suppress the internal criticism needed to solve problems.
biasFlags: Deterministic model that minimizes human agency to change the cycle

entries.source: Why Trump Acts Like a Toddler: A Psychiatrist Explains
entries.summary: Psychiatric analysis suggesting Trump's behavior is rooted in 'developmental arrest' at age two due to the extended absence of his mother.
keyFacts: Identifies 'splitting' (black and white thinking) and 'confabulation' (filling narrative gaps with feelings) as toddler traits in Trump.
Argues Trump seeks 'instant gratification' rather than strategic planning because his psyche remains frozen at an infant stage.
Claims that military school provided discipline but failed to provide the 'reparative relational experience' needed to mature.
biasFlags: Speculative diagnosis of a public figure without clinical examination

entries.source: Why the Worst People Are So Successful
entries.summary: Explores how 'Dark Triad' personality traits (narcissism, Machiavellianism, psychopathy) are uniquely suited for success in large, impersonal, and complex systems.
keyFacts: Moral instincts designed for small groups fail in scaled environments due to distance and abstraction.
Dark Triad individuals are overrepresented in both CEO suites and prisons, indicating a high-risk/high-reward profile.
Guilt and shame are described as 'internal alarm systems' that successful bad actors lack, eventually leading to their self-destruction.

entries.source: Phases of Successful Authoritarian Resistance
entries.summary: Defines a four-phase model for resistance: 1. Recognition, 2. Organization, 3. Escalation, and 4. Transition.
keyFacts: Attorneys General preparing pre-written motions is cited as an example of early 'Recognition' and 'Organization.'
The 'Transition' phase requires reconstructing systems rather than just returning to pre-authoritarian norms.
Highlights the importance of 'listening to the canaries' (marginalized groups) who experience systemic failure first.

entries.source: Is MAGA really a fascist movement?
entries.summary: Compares modern MAGA rhetoric to Mussolini's original fascist ideology, focusing on the fusion of state and corporate power and the cult of national purity.
keyFacts: Fascism is defined as a 'cult of national purity' that flattens diverse local cultures into a singular image of the leader.
Mussolini specifically targeted unions and working-class unity as 'divisive' to the war effort.
Traces the hijacking of Giuseppe Mazzini's vision of national unification into a tool for regional domination.

entries.source: Why 'Humans' Are Better Than We Think | Frankly 108
entries.summary: Discusses how modern social structures amplify the behaviors of a 'psychopathic tail' of the population, despite the median human being inherently cooperative.
keyFacts: Clinical psychopaths make up roughly 1% of males and 0.25% of females.
The transition to agriculture allowed aggressive individuals to hoard lootable surplus, shifting the game theory toward 'Hawks.'
As social trust falls, reciprocal altruism is replaced by expensive contracts and surveillance systems.

entries.source: On Conservatism
entries.summary: Argues that modern conservatism has shifted from its Burkean roots of 'societal stability' to a mechanism for protecting elite privilege via culture wars.
keyFacts: U.S. conservatism is framed as historically upholding traditions of slavery and segregation under the guise of 'liberty.'
Culture wars are described as distractions used to pass economic policies (like the 2017 tax cuts) that favor billionaires.
The emotional appeal of conservatism (belonging, order) is used to lead individuals to vote against their material interests.
biasFlags: Strong progressive/critical theory framing of conservative history

entries.source: On Why the Stock Market Doesnt Matter
entries.summary: Critiques the use of the stock market as a national prosperity metric, highlighting how buybacks and tax loopholes benefit only the top 10%.
keyFacts: Stock buybacks artificially inflate Earnings Per Share (EPS) without creating actual value or jobs.
The 1982 deregulation of buybacks shifted the social contract from reinvestment to 'shareholder value.'
Introduces 'collateralized borrowing' as a primary method for the wealthy to live tax-free by borrowing against asset growth.

entries.source: Effective Resistance to Authoritarianism: The violets in the mountains have broken the rocks
entries.summary: Emphasizes non-violence and mass participation as the only effective ways to erode an authoritarian regime's support base.
keyFacts: Research suggests that sustained engagement from 3.5% of the population can lead to regime change.
Non-violence is strategic; it denies the regime the 'justification' for violent crackdowns.
Proposes offering enablers 'off-ramps'—graceful exits that allow them to defect without public humiliation.

entries.source: The Rise of Technofascists, with Sam Harris | The David Frum Show
entries.summary: Discusses the shift in Silicon Valley toward authoritarian politics and the dangerous intersection of technology, politics, and public health.
keyFacts: U.S. federal prosecutors are uniquely susceptible to political interference because they are presidential appointees, unlike in Germany or Canada.
A vocal minority of tech leaders have embraced 'techno-authoritarian' ideologies (e.g., Curtis Yarvin) as a backlash against 'wokeness.'
The 'Make America Healthy Again' (MAHA) movement is characterized as a political ideology that prioritizes individual choice over collective health science.

entries.source: America is an Anti-Civilization - Prof. Jiang Xueqin
entries.summary: Uses Alexis de Tocqueville to argue that America was designed as an 'anti-civilization'—a transparent game for material acquisition rather than a cultural identity.
keyFacts: Tocqueville feared that a focus on liberty and the middle class would lead to a world that is 'atomized, uniform, and mediocre.'
The 'tyranny of the majority' in democratic societies often suppresses great individuals and original thought.
The MAGA movement is interpreted as an attempt to restore the idea of 'Civilization' (White Christian Democracy) over the American 'Game' (Materialism).
biasFlags: Subjective interpretation of the MAGA movement's core intent

entries.source: Buy Nothing - The Secret Weapon Your Government Accidentally Created
entries.summary: Advocates for the 'Buy Nothing' strategy as a way to exploit the consumer system's fundamental weakness: its total dependence on participation.
keyFacts: Post-WWII government policies (like the GI Bill) were intentionally designed to create a consumer-driven economy.
U.S. household debt has exceeded $18 trillion, turning citizens into 'modern serfs' to the financial system.
The 'Gift Economy' (sharing resources locally) reduces demand for corporate goods and builds community resilience.

entries.source: How do tell if youre winning against fascism - even when it doesnt feel like it.
entries.summary: Proposes four metrics for measuring the success of pro-democracy movements under authoritarian regimes to prevent demoralization.
keyFacts: Success metrics: 1. Increased unity, 2. Growing movement size, 3. Proliferation of tactics, 4. Growing regime disapproval.
As of October 2025, pro-democracy enrollment in groups like Indivisible is growing weekly.
Authoritarian psychological warfare aims to make resistance feel futile, even when progress is measurable.
biasFlags: Assumes 'pro-democracy' and 'anti-Trump' are synonymous

entries.source: Want to protest? You need to learn The CLARA Method
entries.summary: Details a specific technique for de-escalating verbal confrontations during political actions.
keyFacts: CLARA: Check-in (Humanity), Listen (Vent), Affirm (Validate feelings), Respond (Open questions), Add (Information).
Uses the 'ABCD' method for situational awareness: Assess, Breathe, Choose, De-escalate.
Argues that de-escalation is similar to non-authoritarian parenting, as heightened adults often regress to childlike responses.

entries.source: The Reason White Women Vote Trump Is Sicker Than We Thought
entries.summary: Analyzes the 'patriarchal bargain' where white women support conservative hierarchies in exchange for proximity to power and perceived safety.
keyFacts: Kate Man describes a 'moral economy of misogyny' where conformity is rewarded and defiance is punished.
Conservative feminism uses the language of 'choice' to defend patriarchal outcomes.
Trump's appeal to this demographic is framed as a protection contract against 'disgusting' external threats (immigrants, feminists).
biasFlags: Highly ideological framing of female voting patterns

entries.source: Whats the best way to lift people out of poverty?
entries.summary: Examines the effectiveness of unconditional direct cash transfers compared to traditional bureaucratic aid.
keyFacts: A 2018 Kenya study showed that a $500 transfer resulted in a 65% increase in business revenue.
Direct cash giving recognizes that individuals experiencing poverty understand their own needs better than philanthropists.
Wealthy countries and private foundations currently hold $1.7 trillion—sufficient to eliminate extreme poverty globally.

entries.source: The Communists Are Coming - A Visual Manifesto | RCI Documentary
entries.summary: Manifesto for a global revolutionary communist movement, arguing that capitalism's systemic crisis prevents any meaningful reform.
keyFacts: Claims that a tiny minority in private boardrooms makes all major global decisions, bypassing democratic input.
Asserts the U.S. already has the productive capacity for an 'economy of abundance' if democratically planned.
Identifies 'identity politics' and 'liberal feminism' as tools that have failed to secure actual material gains for workers.
biasFlags: Explicitly revolutionary and partisan ideological framing

entries.source: The Secret Plan To Tank Zohran
entries.summary: Analyzes the massive Super PAC spending intended to defeat socialist candidates like Zoran Mamdani, citing the 2021 Buffalo mayoral race as a precedent.
keyFacts: In 2021, the Buffalo establishment funded a write-in campaign for Byron Brown to defeat the socialist primary winner India Walton.
Super PACs like 'Fix the City' and 'Housing for All' (funded by landlords) have spent tens of millions against Mamdani.
Mamdani's campaign relied on over 54,000 small-dollar donations, contrasting with the corporate funding of his opponents.
biasFlags: Strong partisan support for the candidate in question

entries.source: Carl Sagan's Guide to Not Being Fooled
entries.summary: Revisits Carl Sagan's 'Baloney Detection Kit' as a tool for navigating modern pseudoscience and disinformation.
keyFacts: Arguments from authority carry little weight; experts can and do err.
Hypotheses must be falsifiable to be scientifically valid.
Information outlets often avoid publicizing the debunking of hoaxes (like crop circles) because 'skepticism doesn't sell.'

entries.source: How Existential Dread Has Shaped the World with Sheldon Solomon | TGS 199
entries.summary: Explains Terror Management Theory (TMT), arguing that human awareness of death drives tribalism, consumption, and political polarization.
keyFacts: Mortality salience (reminders of death) causes individuals to cling more fiercely to their cultural worldviews.
Experiments show that reminders of death make people more punitive toward those who challenge their beliefs.
Consumerism provides a sense of psychological security and 'symbolic immortality' through wealth.

entries.source: Grey Rocking: Your superpower against MAGA and Trump (part 1)
entries.summary: Advocates for 'Grey Rocking'—becoming uninteresting and unresponsive—as a strategy to disrupt the inflammatory 'baiting' tactics used by political provocateurs.
keyFacts: Right-wing streamers provoke emotional reactions from protesters to generate profitable content.
Proposes playing copyrighted Disney music near streamers to trigger automatic content flags and demonetization.
Grey rocking deprives 'malignant narcissists' of the emotional fuel they need to justify authoritarian crackdowns.
biasFlags: Applies clinical psychology terms to a broad political movement

entries.source: no one knows how to act anymore. there's a reason why.
entries.summary: Uses Sartre's concept of 'seriality' to explain how the internet has fragmented shared reality into distinct, isolated digital worlds.
keyFacts: The internet functions as 'practico-inert'—a human creation that now limits human freedom and consciousness.
Large events (scarcity, pandemics) can either drive 'seriality' (competition) or 'groups in fusion' (collective action).
Cognitive mapping is proposed as a way to understand the systems we are in to regain agency.

entries.source: WATCH: AOC SPILLS THE TEA On Marjorie Greene's Political Switch-Up
entries.summary: Critiques Marjorie Taylor Greene's recent 'populist' rhetoric regarding healthcare as a purely personal 'revenge tour' against the GOP leadership.
keyFacts: Private health insurance premiums have increased by an average of 18% recently.
Small business owners frequently pay $2,000/month for plans with $10,000 deductibles.
Greene's voting record shows consistent opposition to the healthcare initiatives she now rhetorically supports.
biasFlags: Hostile partisan tone

entries.source: WHITE NATIONALIST CLASH IN THE REPUBLICAN PARTY
entries.summary: Hasan Piker analyzes the surge of antisemitism in the GOP as an inevitable result of training a base to despise all 'out-groups.'
keyFacts: Argues that the pro-Israel stance of the State Department is based on military strategy, not 'Jewish power.'
Claims the GOP weaponizes antisemitism to defend Israel while simultaneously gutting anti-discrimination protections for other groups.
Identifies 'Project Esther' (Heritage Foundation) as a plan to silence domestic criticism of Israeli policy.
biasFlags: Strong partisan framing of foreign policy and religious dynamics

entries.source: Shes A Billionaires Worst Nightmare. Now Shes Back.
entries.summary: Discusses the appointment of Lina Khan to the NYC Mayor-elect's transition team as a sign of resistance against corporate power.
keyFacts: Lina Khan is known for using antitrust law to block mergers (e.g., Kroger/Albertson's) and challenge Amazon's market dominance.
Her statement emphasizes protecting small businesses and New Yorkers from 'outside corporate money.'
Notes that NYC's population density acts as a 'natural firewall' against the complete elimination of local economies by big box chains.

entries.source: If We Tax Rich People... They Will Just Leave!
entries.summary: Evaluates 'tax flight' claims, arguing that most developed nations are on the right side of the Laffer Curve, where higher taxes would increase total revenue.
keyFacts: Despite threats of flight, NYC's millionaire population has nearly doubled since 2010.
A study suggests U.S. top marginal rates could reach 76% before significant revenue loss occurs due to emigration.
The economic impact of 1,000 new millionaires is more beneficial than that of one single billionaire.

entries.source: Justin Wolfers: Why the Media Gets the Economy and the Market Wrong
entries.summary: Explains the 'K-shaped economy' where strong GDP growth (4.3%) masks the reality that the bottom 80% are using 'Buy Now, Pay Later' for necessities.
keyFacts: The top 20% of earners account for 63% of all U.S. consumer spending.
The U.S. stock market (18% growth) has underperformed global markets (30%) over the past year.
Trump's plan to garnish wages for student loans in default is identified as a significant 'pro-growth' inhibitor.

entries.source: Peter Thiel and Praxis: The Billionaire Plan to Create the Fourth Reich
entries.summary: Examines 'Praxis' and the pursuit of 'Freedom Cities' as a new form of corporate company town that bypasses democratic law.
keyFacts: Trump proposed a contest to charter 10 brand-new 'Freedom Cities' on public lands.
Praxis aims to establish an 'internet native nation' for the wealthy, with Greenland as a target location.
Venture firm Pronomos Capital (Peter Thiel) funds private city projects in Africa, South Asia, and Honduras.
biasFlags: Includes hyperbolic 'Fourth Reich' framing

entries.source: Private Equity Is A Wall Street Scam
entries.summary: Explains how private equity firms extract value from healthy companies by loading them with debt through 'leveraged buyouts.'
keyFacts: Firms like KKR and Bain extracted $5 billion from Toys R Us, while employees received zero severance in bankruptcy.
Firms often sell a company's real estate to themselves and then charge the company rent for its own buildings.
This extraction model is expanding from retail into the hospital and veterinary sectors.

entries.source: A post-American, enshittification-resistant internet (39C3 Hamburg)
entries.summary: Cory Doctorow argues that U.S. trade policy and anti-circumvention laws (DMCA) have turned American tech into a global security liability.
keyFacts: The DMCA allows manufacturers to prevent the remediation of product defects, described as 'felony contempt of business model.'
The U.S. Cloud Act allows the government to compel U.S. companies to disclose user data stored anywhere in the world.
Advocates for repealing anti-circumvention laws to allow 'digital sovereignty' for other nations via interoperable products.

entries.source: Eff It, Lets Beat AIPAC | Kat Abughazaleh For Illinois
entries.summary: Exposes how AIPAC uses Republican-donor money to influence Democratic primaries, specifically targeting progressive candidates.
keyFacts: AIPAC spent $45 million against Jamal Bowman and Cori Bush in 2024 for calling for a Gaza ceasefire.
AIPAC has labeled candidate Abughazaleh a 'dangerous detractor' for supporting the International Criminal Court.
AIPAC sought to influence 361 individual congressional races in the 2024 cycle.
biasFlags: Self-promotional campaign framing

entries.source: I am a TAD concerned about the future
entries.summary: Argues that modern culture is trapped in a Promethean/Frankenstein dialectic, where science fiction fails to imagine futures outside of technological domination.
keyFacts: Identifies science fiction as the 'mythos of modernity' that has been 'sold to the machine.'
Proposes analyzing psychopolitics and accelerationism to understand how the future was lost to capitalist automation.
Critiques the soul-crushing nature of corporate entertainment as a barrier to authentic myth-making.
biasFlags: Metaphorical and abstract framing of economic systems as 'The Machine'

entries.source: I am somewhat disturbed by how much DUNE resembles...2026
entries.summary: Uses Frank Herbert's 'Dune' to analyze modern power dynamics, drawing parallels between the 'Great Houses' and current geopolitical entities.
keyFacts: Argues that the 0.01% ultra-high net worth group functions as the true board of directors for global capitalism.
Compares 'hydraulic despotism' (control of water) to modern control of data, internet grids, and digital currencies.
Describes the MAGA movement as a group that prefers overt imperialist approaches over the 'benevolent pretense' of previous U.S. foreign policy.
biasFlags: Strong use of literary allegory to interpret current events

entries.source: Nobody Wants To Be Working Class
entries.summary: Critiques traditional labor unions for becoming 'domesticated' by capitalism and advocates for the establishment of 'commons' in housing, energy, and healthcare.
keyFacts: Argues that unions have shifted from radical transformation to short-term negotiation, losing their disruptive power.
Promotes the concept of 'self-abolition of the working class' to move beyond the identity of a 'worker' for profit.
Suggests that deindustrialization and the precariousness of gig work have rendered 20th-century union models less effective.
biasFlags: Explicitly anarchist and anti-capitalist ideological framing

entries.source: Trump's Nobel Prize Letter to Norway Just BACKFIRES Spectacularly
entries.summary: Details how a letter from President Trump to Norway linking the Nobel Peace Prize to trade tariffs has undermined institutional predictability.
keyFacts: The U.S. implemented 10% to 25% tariffs on eight NATO allies (Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, UK, Netherlands, Finland).
EU is reportedly discussing $107 billion in counter-tariffs on American products.
Gold hit all-time highs as central banks began diversifying reserves away from the dollar in response to institutional instability.
biasFlags: Interprets geopolitical maneuvers through a lens of 'collapse of credibility'

entries.source: yes. I feel it too.
entries.summary: Discusses a 'collective recalibration' characterized by widespread fatigue, arguing that modern economic systems treat humans as 'batteries' to power corporate growth.
keyFacts: Claims that a small elite gatekeeps information to prevent the masses from accessing self-actualization.
Argues that humans are born with a 'reserve of life force' (chi) that is being systematically siphoned by current work structures.
Advocates for a shift towards 'vibrational attunement' and 'technologies of the self' to reclaim attention.
biasFlags: Relies on metaphysical/spiritual concepts rather than materialist analysis

entries.source: The Video Trump Doesn't Want You To See
entries.summary: Critiques U.S. foreign policy as a 'hypocritical' pursuit of oil and resources disguised as a defense of democracy.
keyFacts: Claims Trump initiated bombings in Iran, Nigeria, and Venezuela within his first year.
Notes that the U.S. Department of Defense was rhetorically renamed to the 'Department of War' under the current administration.
Asserts the 'Make America Great' narrative is a lie designed to benefit Wall Street at the expense of the global working class.
biasFlags: Highly polemical tone; presents geopolitical analysis as an 'expose' of secret truths

entries.source: UK Green Party Stuns With Huge Surge + Brilliant New Political Ad
entries.summary: Analyzes the rise of the UK Green Party, which has grown to 140,000 members and 16% in polls by focusing on the 'treadmill' of the cost-of-living crisis.
keyFacts: The Green Party is now the third-largest political party in the UK by supporter numbers.
Their latest ad frames economic hardship as a 'political choice' that benefits the super-rich over the public.
Notes disillusionment with the Labour Party under Keir Starmer as a driver for the Green Party's growth.
biasFlags: Strongly positive framing of the Green Party's messaging

entries.source: The Thing About Power that the Powerful Can't Understand
entries.summary: Explores the psychological disconnect of influencers who view themselves as 'ordinary people' while wielding 'influence-sized' power.
keyFacts: Differentiates between 'person-sized' experiences and 'influence-sized' impacts.
Argues that the press serves a vital role in holding influential creators accountable, even when driven by clicks.
Nostalgia for a time without public scrutiny is framed as an unproductive feeling that ignores current power realities.

entries.source: MAGA is crumbling. But not for the reason you think
entries.summary: Argues that the MAGA coalition is fracturing due to economic disillusionment, as working-class voters realize the 'populist' promises have primarily benefited the elite.
keyFacts: Contends that racism is an insufficient explanation for Trump's support, as many former Democrats voted for him seeking material security.
Highlights the election of Zoran Mamdani as evidence of a persistent public appetite for genuine left-wing populism.
Cites cuts to essential services (e.g., tsunami warning systems) alongside subsidies for political allies as evidence of corruption.
biasFlags: Speculative analysis of the 'fracturing' of a political movement

entries.source: Why do so many Americans flinch at this term? Can we change their minds?
entries.summary: Argues that 'socialized medicine' is already a successful reality in the U.S. through the military's TRICARE system.
keyFacts: Claims for-profit healthcare bylaws legally prioritize profits over patient lives, leading to claim denials.
Many members of Congress own stock in healthcare corporations, creating a direct conflict of interest regarding universal healthcare policy.
Proposes a ban on single stock trading for elected officials to remove financial incentives for maintaining the status quo.

entries.source: Anonymous: The Story MSNBC Doesn't Want You to Hear
entries.summary: Discusses the firing of an MSNBC host for linking hate speech to political violence, arguing that corporate media suppresses truth.
keyFacts: Host Matthew Dow was reportedly terminated for stating that hateful rhetoric leads to hate-fueled actions.
Argues that the normalization of violent rhetoric from political leaders increases the frequency of domestic attacks.
Critiques media outlets for prioritizing corporate interests over journalistic integrity.
biasFlags: Includes unverified claims about specific firing reasons

entries.source: WAKE UP ABOUT CULTS!
entries.summary: Explores the 'cult of patriarchy, white supremacy, and capitalism' as an all-encompassing system that removes individual agency through cognitive programming.
keyFacts: Uses the 'Pick Me' culture as an example of how marginalized individuals seek validation from dominant power structures for survival.
Describes Stockholm Syndrome within religious and political cults where members defend their abusers.
Argues that underestimating the intelligence of cult members prevents meaningful deconstruction of the systems that trap them.
biasFlags: Highly ideological and repetitive framing

entries.source: SHAMED BY CULT
entries.summary: Discusses how religious cults use shame to control members and discredit those who leave.
keyFacts: Argues that 'sinful' behavior is often common among active cult members as a coping mechanism for systemic shame.
Uses personal anecdotes from the Mormon faith to illustrate the disconnect between public morality and private actions.
Claims cults falsely tell departing members they are 'only leaving so they can sin.'
biasFlags: Anecdotal and emotionally charged

entries.source: MAGA BREAKING/WH IS A MEGA CHURCH
entries.summary: Compares the MAGA movement to a 'mega-church' model driven by narcissistic abuse and Stockholm Syndrome.
keyFacts: Argues that many on the right misinterpret socialism and communism as synonyms for Nazism due to media figures like Rush Limbaugh.
Describes the current administration as exploiting the brain's need for dopamine to keep followers engaged in a state of 'known horror.'
Notes that former supporters often identify as 'independent' or 'no contact' rather than switching to the left.
biasFlags: Pathologizes political movements as clinical abuse patterns

entries.source: TRUMP PISSED OFF MAGA
entries.summary: Discusses a perceived shift where Trump's softer rhetoric on 'good worker' undocumented immigrants is alienating his 'America First' base.
keyFacts: Supporters expressed anger over a deleted tweet suggesting parental permission was sufficient for transgender surgeries.
Notes that figures like Nick Fuentes are now encouraging followers to 'vote blue' to spite the 'uniparty' GOP.
Claims that under the Biden administration, foreign countries were less cooperative in accepting deportees than under the current administration.
biasFlags: Bases national sentiment shifts on specific social media deletions and far-right podcaster reactions

entries.source: BEING THE CHANGE STARTS WITH ONESELF
entries.summary: Argues that the political left suffers from a 'rage problem' that mirrors the right, hindering the ability to engage with and convert potential allies.
keyFacts: Critiques left-wing content creators for 'rage-baiting' that dehumanizes the opposition.
Suggests that the left's 'moral purity culture' makes individuals feel judged and drives them toward the right.
Advocates for personal emotional regulation as a prerequisite for effective political organizing.
biasFlags: Strong 'both-sides' psychological framing

entries.source: RAPID RESPONSE TEAMS
entries.summary: Argues that Trump's announcements regarding National Guard 'Rapid Response Teams' are largely a rebranding of existing civil rights era riot control protocols.
keyFacts: The National Guard has been trained in riot control since the 1960s; the 'new' 500-member state teams are not a policy novelty.
Claims the administration is 'repurposing' existing structures to appear more authoritarian and decisive.
Urges the public to differentiate between substantive policy shifts and performative 'bluster.'

entries.source: MAGA, GOOGLE IT!
entries.summary: Explains why 'just Googling' doesn't work for individuals in high-control groups, as their brain's threat-detection system views contradictory information as 'stranger danger.'
keyFacts: Cognitive dissonance is felt as a physical threat to identity within cult structures.
Cults train members to 'doubt their doubts' and punish logical inquiry.
Kindness from perceived 'enemies' (e.g., the LGBTQ+ community) is more effective than factual debate in breaking programming.
biasFlags: Uses clinical pathologizing to explain political differences

entries.source: Hustle is the new heroin: a case study of the neoliberal self
entries.summary: Satirizes the 'rise and grind' culture, arguing that 'hustle' is a neoliberal tool for self-exploitation that masks underlying wealth and privilege.
keyFacts: Argues that many 'self-made' entrepreneurs actually rely on parental wealth to fund their initial ventures.
Describes the pressure to perform 'struggle' as a way to hide the comfort that enables ambition.
Connects the hustle mindset to precarious multi-level marketing (MLM) structures.
biasFlags: Highly satirical/opinion-based

entries.source: Let's talk about Trump and hundreds of thousands losing healthcare (it started)....
entries.summary: Discusses the expiration of ACA subsidies, noting that federal data shows 800,000 fewer people selected insurance plans due to rising premiums.
keyFacts: Predominantly Republican states like Louisiana, Mississippi, and Tennessee are predicted to be the most affected by subsidy losses.
Trump has reportedly threatened to veto any attempts to lower health insurance costs for the working class.
Frames the expiration of subsidies as a policy that lowers the tax burden for employers while forcing families to ration care.
biasFlags: Strong partisan framing against the GOP

entries.source: Eric Li: The West Has No Idea Whats Coming
entries.summary: Contrasts Western globalization failures with China's 'socialism with Chinese characteristics,' where capital is kept subordinate to political authority.
keyFacts: China has lifted nearly 100 million people out of absolute poverty in the last decade.
Xi Jinping's anti-corruption campaign is described as the most intense in 2,000 years of Chinese history.
Argues China offers a 'non-ideological' model of globalization that respects national sovereignty over universal liberal values.
biasFlags: Significant pro-CCP/China-centric framing; minimizes human rights concerns

entries.source: Why Zohrans videos are about to change politics forever
entries.summary: Examines Zoran Mamdani's unique campaign media strategy, which uses Bollywood-style editing and viral local content to reach non-English speaking voters.
keyFacts: Highlights 'Halalflation,' where NYC halal cart permit fees of $22,000 inflate the price of food for the working class.
Over half of NYC residents speak a language other than English at home, a demographic targeted by Mamdani's multi-lingual videos.
The campaign focuses on tangible affordability issues: rent freezes, free buses, and universal childcare.
biasFlags: Strongly supportive framing of the candidate

entries.source: Toxic CEO: Replacing Everyone with AI
entries.summary: A thought experiment where a superintelligent AI concludes that the CEO is the most efficient person to fire for maximizing company value.
keyFacts: Defines capitalism as 'the exploitation of labor and the manufacturing of resource scarcity.'
Argues that the CEO's salary is an unnecessary expense compared to the value of the actual labor force.
Suggests the ultimate purpose of a company should be human contribution, not stock prices.
biasFlags: Fictionalized/satirical thought experiment

entries.source: What do Democrats need to do to win? 'Wake up every day and attack Trump'
entries.summary: Former GOP strategists and analysts discuss the need for Democrats to move past 'internal debates' and aggressively attack the material failures of the Trump regime.
keyFacts: Critiques the Democratic establishment for relying on focus groups rather than conviction-driven messaging.
Points to rising egg prices and the arrest of elected officials as evidence that Trump's promises have failed.
Argues that passion and directness are what attract voters to figures like AOC and Bernie Sanders.
biasFlags: Strongly partisan 'attack' strategy advocated

entries.source: CIA psychology: Ask questions that make people reveal everything | Andrew Bustamante
entries.summary: Examines the 'economy of secrets,' arguing that social life is a constant competition for leverage and information masked by the construct of 'fairness.'
keyFacts: Introduces 'elicitation' as the art of gathering info without revealing interest in it.
Advocates for speaking in open-ended questions (How, Why, Tell me about) to extract more data than intended.
Argues that secrets have a 'shelf-life' and should be traded strategically for reciprocity.
biasFlags: Transactional and cynical framing of human relationships

entries.source: Chris Hedges: The Warped Psychology of the Rich
entries.summary: Argues that extreme privilege creates a 'pathology' where the wealthy view other humans as disposable commodities.
keyFacts: Uses the 'Careless People' trope from The Great Gatsby to describe the elite's habit of creating damage and leaving others to clean it up.
Refers to figures like George W. Bush and Trump as beneficiaries of 'insidious affirmative action for the rich.'
Asserts that the primary mechanism of elite control is the control of ideas through corporate-funded culture.
biasFlags: Highly critical, deterministic framing of the wealthy as a class

entries.source: The Greatest Tragedy Is to Be Good in a Rotten World - Nietzsche
entries.summary: Explores Nietzsche's critique of 'herd morality,' arguing that contemporary society punishes genuine goodness and creates 'emotional addiction' disguised as virtue.
keyFacts: Differentiates between passive submission and active, creative goodness.
Argues that 'will to power' is about self-expression and growth, not dominating others.
Warns that resentment often fuels 'apparent goodness,' where individuals criticize the happy to validate their own limitations.

entries.source: Donald Trump's Continued Outbursts Against Canada
entries.summary: Discusses the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada, with Steve Schmidt arguing that Trump's tariffs are 'malicious and punitive.'
keyFacts: A poll from the Angus Reid Institute shows Canadians now view the U.S. as a greater threat than China.
The Save America Movement (SAM) is running ads showing the economic damage (foreclosures) to Americans caused by Canada tariffs.
Notes that Mark Carney is seen as one of the few Western leaders who has successfully 'managed' the Trump relationship.
biasFlags: Strong Never-Trump/GOP-reformist framing

entries.source: You'll never escape life's problems. Why that's actually liberating | Oliver Burkeman
entries.summary: Oliver Burkeman argues that seeking a problem-free life is a recipe for meaninglessness, proposing instead that we 'befriend our gnawing rats' (avoided tasks).
keyFacts: Resonance in life comes from areas where our personal limitations meet objective reality.
Discomfort is framed not as an error, but as a sign that deeply conditioned parts of the self are being challenged.
Suggests working in 'tiny increments' to lower the psychological boundary of daunting tasks.

entries.source: You need to hear what this neuropsych says you were getting wrong.
entries.summary: Connects clinical anxiety to a 'disturbed relationship with the unknown,' arguing that political 'future faking' exploits this need for certainty.
keyFacts: Defines 'future faking' as a manipulation tactic that presents a bleak, predetermined future to stifle resistance.
Asserts that a defeatist mindset is an 'easy way out' for privileged individuals to avoid the work of fighting for change.
Claims freedom requires 'sovereign and unpredictable' thinkers, whereas authoritarianism relies on predictable automatons.
biasFlags: Strong moral judgment of 'defeatist' mindsets

entries.source: How to Speak So Clearly People Assume Youre Brilliant
entries.summary: Outlines five common communication errors and techniques for clear articulation to improve influence and public perception.
keyFacts: Identifies 'Spray and Pray' (overwhelming with data) as a primary communication failure.
Introduces the 'Structure First Rule' to provide a mental map for listeners before explaining complex ideas.
Advocates for 'Express Mode' over 'Impress Mode'—shifting focus from sounding smart to ensuring audience understanding.

entries.source: The right-wing have gone f***n insane
entries.summary: Argues that modern right-wing influencers adopt a 'Joker' archetype, using misinformation and role inversion to appear anti-establishment while serving elite interests.
keyFacts: Traces the history of Batman villains as parodies of 1940s anti-establishment radicalism.
Identifies Harry Frankfurt's definition of 'bullshit' as info known to be false by both producer and consumer, central to the 'Joker-right' business model.
Posits that figures like Nick Fuentes represent the logical 'mask-off' end of conservative dog-whistling.
biasFlags: Uses strong emotive and polemical language to characterize political opposition

entries.source: The Collapse of Open Inquiry: Sacred Victims and Forbidden Questions
entries.summary: Discusses the perceived impact of 'woke cultural socialism' on institutions, arguing it prioritizes equal outcomes and emotional safety over objective truth.
keyFacts: Defines 'Woke' as the making sacred of historically marginalized identity groups.
Critiques 'disparate impact' law for attributing all outcome differences solely to discrimination.
Argues that extreme political polarization has led to 'filter bubbles' among the highly educated elite.
biasFlags: Highly critical framing of social justice movements as a form of institutional corruption

entries.source: What actually makes a good life, according to 85 years of data | Robert Waldinger
entries.summary: Presents findings from the longest study on adult development, highlighting that relational quality is the strongest predictor of long-term health and happiness.
keyFacts: The Harvard Study of Adult Development has tracked over 2,000 individuals across 724 families for 85 years.
Estimates that 40% of happiness is within an individual's control through conscious relational choices.
Loneliness is identified as a significant physical stressor that elevates cortisol and contributes to systemic breakdown.

entries.source: DREDD: sexy fascists
entries.summary: Analyzes the Judge Dredd character as a satire of fascism that audiences often misinterpret as a heroic archetype due to a subconscious attraction to order.
keyFacts: Judge Dredd was originally created as a parody of the 'Nazi uniform' aesthetic to critique authoritarian state violence.
Contrasts the 2012 film 'Dredd' with the 1990s 'America' comic storyline, where Dredd is portrayed explicitly as a monster.
Argues that the ironic framing of violent characters often fails as it inadvertently normalizes fascist worldviews.
biasFlags: Leans heavily on Freudian psychological interpretations of political desire

entries.source: How to Start a Revolution in 2026
entries.summary: Roger Hallam outlines a strategic blueprint for a people-powered movement focusing on local 'bread and butter' issues to bypass failing state institutions.
keyFacts: Proposes a 'fast and furious' mobilization model to capitalize on inevitable societal ruptures caused by debt and climate change.
Uses the 'labor dynamo' vs 'money dynamo' feedback loops as the engine for movement growth.
Advocates for 'Assembly Government' as a participatory replacement for traditional bourgeois political parties.
biasFlags: Explicitly revolutionary; dismisses traditional representative democracy as 'bankrupt'

entries.source: Is ANTIFAscism really a violent, extremist movement?
entries.summary: Explores the historical and philosophical lineage of anti-fascism, arguing it is a universal human impulse rooted in opposition to the 'fasces' of Roman power.
keyFacts: Defines fascism through five elements: statism, militarization, theocracy, corporatism, and ethno-nationalism.
Traces the 'Antifa' name to late-1920s Germany as a defense of working people against Nazi violence.
Frames historical figures like Socrates and Jesus as proto-anti-fascists for prioritizing conscience over state authority.
biasFlags: Highly idealized framing of anti-fascism as a trans-historical moral constant

entries.source: why feeling informed isn't being informed (goose explains)
entries.summary: Explains the 'illusion of explanatory depth,' where modern information consumption leads to the performance of knowledge without genuine comprehension.
keyFacts: Algorithms prioritize 'cognitive ease' and emotional reactions like outrage over complex understanding.
Shares that social knowledge often functions as a signal of group belonging rather than a pursuit of accuracy.
Advocates for 'slowing down' and attempting to explain topics out loud to reveal gaps in comprehension.

entries.source: 6 Key Strategies for De-escalating at Protests and Beyond
entries.summary: Introduces the 'Six Ds of De-escalation' as critical skills for maintaining non-violent discipline during political actions.
keyFacts: The Six Ds: Direct Intervention, Distancing, Delay, Distraction, Delegation, Documentation.
Emphasizes that documentation (filming) can encourage self-control in escalated individuals through external accountability.
Highlights the need for 'role-playing' to build muscle memory for these skills under high-stress conditions.

entries.source: They started an innovative movement to protect businesses from ICE
entries.summary: Details the 'Signs of Solidarity' project which uses legal distinctions between warrant types to protect immigrant workers from ICE.
keyFacts: Teaches business owners that ICE cannot enter 'private employee-only areas' without a judicial warrant.
Administrative warrants are identified as insufficient for entering non-public spaces without owner consent.
Uses local sign displays as a method of 'holding the line' for more vulnerable immigrant-owned businesses.

entries.source: For MAGA, it's not that complicated
entries.summary: Critiques the Republican framing of the U.S. as a 'republic, not a democracy' as a strategic move to justify ignoring constituent needs.
keyFacts: Argues that a constitutional republic is a specific form of democracy, not an alternative to it.
Claims that undermining public education has made the populace more susceptible to simplistic 'Republic = GOP' framing.
States that Republican legislators are increasingly handing over 'power of the purse' and constitutional authority to the executive branch.
biasFlags: Interprets GOP rhetorical framing as a deliberate long-term strategy of intellectual suppression

entries.source: Trumps FAR-RIGHT Project 2025 is Halfway Complete
entries.summary: Discusses the implementation of Project 2025 goals during a government shutdown, focusing on the centralization of executive power.
keyFacts: Public opinion indicates a majority of Americans blame the Republican party for current government shutdowns.
Notes that the U.S. economy is increasingly fragile due to wealth and spending concentration in the top 10%.
Project 2025 aims to dismantle the administrative state and place judicial administration under presidential control.
biasFlags: Uses alarmist economic framing; strong anti-administration stance

entries.source: How My Congressional Campaign Is Feeding People Right Now
entries.summary: Kat Abughazaleh argues for a 'service-based' campaign model that uses resources for direct mutual aid rather than consultant fees.
keyFacts: Replaced high-priced ticket entry with menstrual product donations for campaign events.
Uses campaign infrastructure to stock community fridges and fund high school mutual aid grants.
Argues that visible material help is more effective for voter engagement than traditional political advertisements.
biasFlags: Self-promotional campaign content

entries.source: What Are The Core Beliefs Of Progressivism? - Making Politics Simple
entries.summary: Provides a primer on the historical and ideological foundations of the progressive movement.
keyFacts: Core tenets include social justice, government regulation of capital, and comprehensive public services.
Differentiates progressives from traditional liberals by their favor for 'active government intervention' over mere preservation of norms.
Traces the ideology back to early 20th-century reforms like women's suffrage and antitrust legislation.

entries.source: WISER PEOPLE TALKING ABOUT PURPLE REVOLUTION
entries.summary: Critiques the 'authoritarian left' for adopting identity-based outrage culture that alienates moderate voters and fuels the far-right.
keyFacts: Introduces 'Identity Substitution'—deriving moral superiority from activism rather than seeking genuine political change.
Argues 'Cognitive Closure' indicates a preference for simple binary heroes and villains over nuanced policy discussion.
Notes that the majority of people fall into a 'middle ground' that is pushed away by the perceived judgment of activist circles.
biasFlags: Internal ideological critique using psychological pathologizing

entries.source: HATE MAKES IT ALL WORSE
entries.summary: Advocates for a shift from confrontational political tactics toward a strategy of listening and understanding underlying motivations.
keyFacts: Argues that characterizing all Trump support as racism is a failed strategy that prevents political conversion.
Promotes 'Purple Revolution' principles of seeking common ground to solve systemic societal issues.
Claims that managing one's own rage is a prerequisite for effective cross-partisan engagement.
biasFlags: Strongly leans toward centrist 'bridge-building' as a primary political tool

entries.source: NICK FUENTES/KID ROCK
entries.summary: Critiques the perceived lack of authenticity and aesthetic appeal of right-wing cultural events, focusing on Kid Rock's halftime show.
keyFacts: Nick Fuentes is cited as agreeing with the assessment of Turning Point USA events as 'fake' or 'cringe.'
The 'All-American Halftime Show' is presented as a 'token celebrity' performance that fails to represent genuine patriotism.
Reflects on the decline of specific cultural segments that no longer resonate with a broader audience.
biasFlags: Relies heavily on subjective aesthetic judgment and 'cringe' culture analysis

entries.source: TRUMP/HITLER SIMILARITIES
entries.summary: Draws historical parallels between the rise of Donald Trump and Adolf Hitler, focusing on family name changes and criminal backgrounds.
keyFacts: Trump's grandfather changed the family name from 'Drumpf' to sound more American.
Hitler's father changed his name from 'Schicklgruber.'
Both figures are characterized as using political office to avoid legal prosecution or deportation.
biasFlags: Strong use of the 'Hitler' analogy which often simplifies complex political differences

entries.source: The BIG LIE - Tim Dillon
entries.summary: Tim Dillon critiques the modern narrative of individual achievement, arguing that 'happiness' is a marketed lie that misdirects people from community.
keyFacts: Argues that the entrepreneurship narrative is a 'profitable lie' that ignores the role of geography and background.
True happiness is observed to derive from social commitments and things that outlast the individual.
Bill Gates is used as an example of wealth not equating to personal fulfillment or 'fun.'
biasFlags: Highly cynical and satirical framing

entries.source: utopia is doomed because of you!
entries.summary: Analyzes how unchecked individualism undermines both capitalist and socialist systems.
keyFacts: In large globalized economies, individual benefit from resource theft often outweighs collective loss.
Resistance to masks during a pandemic is used as an example of 'selfish individualism' being confused with 'freedom.'
Uses a 'cake analogy' to show how bad actors undermine any economic distribution system.

entries.source: Seth Godin Gets Personal About Strategy in This Unique Interview
entries.summary: Seth Godin discusses strategy as a personal philosophy of growth, emphasizing agency and the rejection of manipulative systems.
keyFacts: Strategy is defined as making choices today that shape the person one will become.
The 'Who's it for? What's it for?' framework is proposed to identify the true purpose of actions.
Generosity is presented as a competitive strategy to overcome nihilism fostered by selfish systems.

entries.source: Facts Don't Win Fights: Heres How to Cut Through Confirmation Bias | Tali Sharot
entries.summary: Explains the neuroscience of confirmation bias, showing that brains 'switch off' when encountering information that contradicts prior beliefs.
keyFacts: Providing more data on climate change often causes further polarization rather than consensus.
Intelligent individuals are more likely to manipulate data to fit pre-existing conclusions.
Effective persuasion relies on finding shared motives (e.g., child health) rather than direct factual confrontation.

entries.source: Robert Anton Wilson Explains Human Nature (1991)
entries.summary: Robert Anton Wilson explores how cultural conditioning and insecurity lead to a 'wired and nervous' society based on criticizing others.
keyFacts: Psychologist Albert Ellis posits a core program in many: 'I am a no-good [expletive],' leading to defensive denunciation of others.
Western society's nervous temperament is traced back to historical dependencies on sugar and coffee.
Criticizes the 'War on Drugs' as a hypocritical tool for social control rather than health.
biasFlags: Contains counter-cultural and unconventional historical theories

entries.source: Andor: a Marxist story
entries.summary: Analyzes the Star Wars series 'Andor' as an allegory for Marxist class consciousness, alienation, and Leninist vanguardism.
keyFacts: Applies Brecht's 'Alienation Effect' to the show's storytelling techniques.
Cassian Andor's search for his sister is interpreted as a search for a 'lost self' alienated by industrial capitalism.
The Narkina 5 prison is analyzed using Michel Foucault's 'Discipline and Punish' framework for creating a compliant workforce.
biasFlags: Strong interpretive bias through a singular ideological lens

entries.source: TOP 10 NORMAN FINKELSTEIN THUG LIFE MOMENTS ON PALESTINE-ISRAEL!
entries.summary: A compilation of Norman Finkelstein's arguments regarding the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, international law, and the siege of Gaza.
keyFacts: Asserts that the Gaza blockade constitutes 'illegal collective punishment' under international law.
Finkelstein defends his scholarship by citing Raul Hilberg, the leading authority on the Holocaust.
Describes the October 7th attackers as individuals raised in what is effectively a 'concentration camp.'
biasFlags: Content is highly polemical and inflammatory

entries.source: Your Mind is an Excellent Servant, but a Terrible Master - David Foster Wallace
entries.summary: Visualization of David Foster Wallace's 'This is Water' speech, focusing on conscious choice and the default setting of self-centeredness.
keyFacts: The value of education is defined as the ability to choose what to pay attention to.
Unconscious 'worship' (of power, beauty, or intellect) is insidious and leads to a sense of perpetual inadequacy.
Freedom is defined as attention, awareness, discipline, and the ability to sacrifice for others.

entries.source: Why Leadership Rewards Narcissists, Liars, and Fools - Machiavelli Knew This
entries.summary: Analyzes why selection systems favor overconfident and narcissistic traits despite their long-term negative impact on organizations.
keyFacts: The 'Prattfall Effect' shows that competent people who show minor flaws are more likable than flawless ones.
CEOs who use simple language are more likely to be hired but more likely to underperform in complex environments.
Proposes mandatory trial periods and the separation of 'proposal' vs 'approval' powers to mitigate narcissistic influence.

entries.source: Mo Gawdat: AIs Biggest Threat Isnt Technology - Its Us
entries.summary: Former Google X executive Mo Gawdat argues that AI disruption will last 12-15 years, with the primary threat being human misuse.
keyFacts: ChatGPT 3.5 already scored in the top 10% of human intelligence with an estimated IQ of 152.
Predicts a dystopian transition phase where AI benefits are concentrated entirely among capitalists.
Envisions AI intelligence reaching thousands of points, enabling molecular reorganization and solving climate change.
biasFlags: High degree of speculation regarding 'molecular manufacturing'

entries.source: What Should Democratic Resistance Look Like?: Kat Abughazaleh Talks Elon + Democrats w/ Jen Psaki
entries.summary: Discusses the threat of corporate data control and argues for an aggressive Democratic response to the Trump-Musk alliance.
keyFacts: Highlights a SCOTUS ruling that allows 'DOGE' access to social security data.
Argues that the Democratic playbook of 'not alienating voters' is ineffective against authoritarian movements.
Mentions Trump contracting with Peter Thiel's AI surveillance companies as a primary concern.
biasFlags: Strong partisan 'resistance' framing

entries.source: Stop Fighting Reality - Learn Radical Acceptance (DBT Skills)
entries.summary: Introduces Radical Acceptance as a tool to stop the psychological 'fight' against facts, which reduces suffering and enables action.
keyFacts: Radical Acceptance is a core distress tolerance skill in Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT).
Non-acceptance of pain creates 'suffering' by adding distressing thoughts to the original event.
Differentiates between accepting 'facts' (past/present) and accepting 'predictions' (the future).

entries.source: New Leftists Need These 7 Habits
entries.summary: Provides a practical guide for moving from 'online posting' to effective real-world political organizing.
keyFacts: Habit 3: Discipline media consumption; compares algorithmic feeds to 'dirty bulk' nutrition.
Habit 7: Practice 'crop rotation' by balancing intense political work with personal life to prevent burnout.
Argues that effective organizers must specialize in one skill rather than trying to do everything.

entries.source: Tim Minchins Full Speech at The Art of Tax Reform Summit 2025 - NSW, Australia
entries.summary: Critiques a culture of risk-aversion and mediocrity, using the Sydney Opera House as a metaphor for building a flourishing society.
keyFacts: Defines art as 'content plus intent,' arguing that AI-generated work lacks the human intent necessary to endure.
Proposes a 'railway' metaphor for the arts: a long-term infrastructure project requiring public investment.
Argues that empathy, expanded through storytelling, is the primary mechanism that sustains liberal democracy.

entries.source: Michael Flynn Wants To Sell Your Blood | Kat For Illinois Campaign Comedy Show
entries.summary: Investigates Michael Flynn's 'For the Pure' website, a platform for unvaccinated individuals seeking bodily fluids and jobs.
keyFacts: Site offered unvaccinated blood, semen, and breast milk for $17.76/month.
Flynn's site partnered with 'America's Future' and 'Children's Health Defense.'
Site collected social security numbers 24 times via email before disappearing in May 2024.
biasFlags: Strong partisan framing; treats the subject as a punchline for a comedy-based campaign event

entries.source: New Data Shows WHY Zohran Mamdani Is So Popular
entries.summary: Analyzes poll data showing broad support for Mamdani's policies, like universal childcare and rent freezes, across party lines.
keyFacts: YouGov poll indicates Americans widely approve of a $30 minimum wage by 2030 and eliminated bus fares.
Mamdani's policies remain popular even when not attributed to his name.
Critiques New York Times for using sources linked to race science for a 'hit piece' on Mamdani.
biasFlags: Openly supportive of the candidate; dismisses opposing reporting as 'hit pieces'

entries.source: Obama Issues DIRE Warning For Democrats | The Kyle Kulinski Show
entries.summary: Kyle Kulinski critiques Barack Obama's private calls for Democrats to 'toughen up,' arguing the former president should be more publicly vocal.
keyFacts: Obama urged less 'whining' and more active defense of free speech at a private fundraiser.
Kulinski argues Democrats should focus on anti-billionaire and anti-fascist narratives rather than zoning reform.
Critiques Abigail Spanberger's campaign as outdated for prioritizing a bipartisan record over radical stance shifts.
biasFlags: Strong ideological preference for radical/populist leftism over pragmatism

entries.source: A WOMAN PRES WOULD ALMOST FOR SURE COME FROM THE RIGHT FIRST
entries.summary: Challenges the assumption that the first female president will be a Democrat, arguing that the Republican base votes as a disciplined bloc.
keyFacts: Claims the right would have supported Sarah Palin or Nikki Haley for President.
Argues the first female president could potentially be a Black woman from the GOP.
Contends that many on the right do not identify as misogynistic despite external perceptions.
